chapter_title.ar: باب مِيرَاثِ ذَوِي الْأَرْحَامِ مَعَ الْمَوَالِي
chapter_title.en: Chapter 32 – Inheritance of the relatives with the Mawali (slave)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (23/201)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (23/201)
text: مَاتَ مَوْلًى لِعَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ ( عليه السلام ) فَقَالَ انْظُرُوا هَلْ تَجِدُونَ لَهُ وَارِثاً فَقِيلَ لَهُ ابْنَتَانِ بِالْيَمَامَةِ مَمْلُوكَتَانِ فَاشْتَرَاهُمَا مِنْ مَالِ مَوْلَاهُ الْمَيِّتِ ثُمَّ دَفَعَ إِلَيْهِمَا بَقِيَّةَ الْمَالِ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Isa, from Abu Sabit, from hanan, from Ibn Abu Yafour, from Is’haq Bin Ammar,

Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> has said: ‘A slave of Ali<sup>asws</sup> Bin Al-Husayn<sup>asws</sup> died, so he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Look around, can you find an inheritor for him?’ So it was said to him<sup>asws</sup>, ‘There are two daughters of his at Yamama, both slaves’. So he<sup>asws</sup> bought them both from the wealth of the dead slave, then handed over the rest to them both’.
en.sarwar: 8. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from abu Thabit from Hanan from ibn abu ibn abu Ya’fur from Ishaq ibn ‘Ammar who has narrated the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that a slave of Ali ibn al-Husayn, ‘Alayhim al-Salam, died. He asked to ascertain if he has left any relatives. It was said that he has two daughters in Yamamah who are owned. He (the Imam) bought them from the legacy of his slave who had died, then gave them the rest of the legacy.”’
